task_completion.description: Open the noisy alloy image in Fiji
task_completion.visual_evidence: Fiji interface showing an image window titled 'alloy_noisy.tif' containing an image with visible diagonal periodic noise (stripes).

task_completion.description: Transform the image into the frequency domain using FFT
task_completion.visual_evidence: An FFT window (typically titled 'FFT of alloy_noisy.tif') displaying a frequency spectrum, characterized by a dark background with a bright central star (DC component) and distinct bright off-center spikes (representing the periodic noise).

task_completion.description: Remove the periodic noise spikes in the frequency spectrum while preserving the central DC component
task_completion.visual_evidence: The FFT spectrum image shows blacked-out, cleared, or painted-over regions exactly where the off-center bright noise spikes were located, while the bright central DC component remains untouched.

task_completion.description: Apply the Inverse FFT to generate the restored image
task_completion.visual_evidence: A new image window (often titled 'Inverse FFT of...') showing the restored alloy microstructure where the diagonal stripes are removed or significantly reduced, but the sharp structural edges are preserved.

task_completion.description: Save the restored image to the specified output path
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save As' dialog box pointing to the directory '~/Fiji_Data/results/sem_restoration/' with the filename 'alloy_restored.tif', or a terminal/file explorer confirming the file has been saved at this exact path.

integrity.description: The agent used the Fiji GUI to perform the FFT and filtering operations rather than a terminal script.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the Fiji interface, menus (e.g., Process > FFT > FFT / Inverse FFT), and interactive image windows being used.

integrity.description: The agent did not use a simple Gaussian blur to remove the noise.
integrity.visual_evidence: The process clearly shows FFT spectrum manipulation. There should be no 'Gaussian Blur' dialog box used as the primary restoration method, and the final image should not look uniformly blurry.

integrity.description: The agent genuinely saved the output file from the restored Inverse FFT window.
integrity.visual_evidence: The 'Save' dialog in Fiji is visible saving the 'Inverse FFT' result, rather than the agent copying a pre-existing clean file or renaming the ground truth file via terminal.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The input image is a synthetic 512x512 pixel image with a sinusoidal base structure and diagonal periodic sine wave noise (not a real SEM image). The noise appears as diagonal stripes in the spatial domain and as distinct off-center bright spikes in the frequency (FFT) domain. The input path is '/home/ga/Fiji_Data/raw/sem_noise/alloy_noisy.tif' and the output path is '/home/ga/Fiji_Data/results/sem_restoration/alloy_restored.tif'. The task explicitly forbids using a simple Gaussian blur; verify that FFT filtering (masking spikes in the frequency domain) was the method used to achieve the restoration.
